Fix two build failures breaking Vercel deployments

1. app/auth/signup/page.tsx — Next.js 15 changed searchParams to a Promise
   in server components. Make component async and await searchParams.

2. lib/stripe.ts — Stripe client was instantiated at module level with `new
   Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!, ...)`. During build STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
   is absent, causing "Neither apiKey nor config.authenticator provided" and
   crashing the /api/stripe/webhook route collection step.
   Fix: lazy-initialize via a Proxy so the client is only constructed on the
   first actual request, not at import time.

commit.message:
Add full Stripe subscription lifecycle management

Webhook handler (app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts):
- checkout.session.completed  → set plan + save stripe_customer_id / subscription_id
- customer.subscription.updated → sync plan on upgrade/downgrade
- customer.subscription.deleted → immediately downgrade to free on cancellation
- invoice.payment_failed → downgrade to free on failed payment
- Signature verified via STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET before any processing
- Falls back to stripe_customer_id lookup when supabase_user_id not in metadata

Checkout session creator (app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts):
- Auth-gated (Bearer token); reuses existing Stripe Customer or creates one
- Saves stripe_customer_id to profile immediately after customer creation
- Embeds supabase_user_id in both session.metadata and subscription_data.metadata
- Supports allow_promotion_codes for future promo code feature

Stripe helpers (lib/stripe.ts):
- planFromPriceId() reverse-maps price IDs to plan slugs
- Renamed price env vars to STRIPE_PRICE_BASIC/SUPER/SHOGUN_MONTHLY

SQL migration (supabase/migrations/001_add_stripe_columns.sql):
- Adds stripe_customer_id (UNIQUE) and stripe_subscription_id (UNIQUE) to profiles
- Adds index on stripe_customer_id for webhook fallback lookup

.env.example: updated with new price ID var names + NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(hooks): allow cd to main repo root when running in worktree (#284)

* fix(hooks): allow cd to main repo root when running in worktree

When the hook script runs inside a git worktree, PROJECT_ROOT resolves
to the worktree path instead of the main repository root. This causes
`cd /path/to/main/repo` to be incorrectly flagged as "outside project
root". Detect worktree via the .git file and also accept the main
repository root as a valid cd target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(hooks): analyze all command segments in bashlex fallback path

The previous fallback only inspected the first segment when bashlex
failed to parse (e.g. heredoc syntax). This allowed bypass via chained
commands like `gh pr list && eval "malicious"` or `curl x | sh`.

Now splits on &&, ||, ;, | and checks every segment for dangerous
patterns including pipe-to-shell detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

SPRINT: SPRINT-GITHUB-LINEAR-INTEGRATION
LINEAR: UNI-14

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): eliminate workbench flash, API spam, and toast storm on reload/HMR

Five root causes fixed:

1. authChecked reset on onMount — Svelte 5 HMR preserves $state across hot
   reloads; resetting to false at the top of onMount ensures the loading screen
   always shows until auth.refresh() resolves, preventing workbench from
   rendering with stale unauthenticated state.

2. Profile $effect re-entry guard — plain (non-reactive) _profileFetching flag
   prevents concurrent fetchAuthMe() calls when auth.accessToken changes due to
   proactive token rotation. Also removes toast.error on profile failure since
   the redirect flow already handles the unauthenticated case.

3. GitHub $effect re-entry guard — same pattern with _githubFetching prevents
   redundant fetchGitHubAuthStatus() + fetchGitHubRepos() calls on each token
   rotation.

4. Toast deduplication — module-level Map tracks last-shown timestamp per
   type:message key; duplicate toasts within a 5 s window are suppressed,
   eliminating the "Profile load failed" storm during auth handshake.

5. auth.refresh() AbortController timeout — 8 s timeout prevents the silent
   refresh from hanging indefinitely when the backend is slow to start, ensuring
   authChecked = true fires promptly and the login screen appears.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(policy): bridge evaluate_policy_rules to policy_dsl engine

Replace the imperative rule matching and sorting logic in
evaluate_policy_rules() with the compiled policy_dsl engine, completing
the bridge from the property-tested DSL compiler (5eae8b0d) into the
production evaluation path.

Before: evaluate_policy_rules manually iterated rules, called
matches_rule() per rule, collected candidates, sorted by
priority/severity/specificity, and picked the best match.

After: rules are compiled to DSL predicates via
policy_dsl::compile_policy_rule(), evaluated via
policy_dsl::evaluate_dsl_rules() which handles matching, ranking, and
selection in one pass, and the result is mapped back to the original
PolicyRule structs.

Safety: debug_assert_eq verifies that the old matches_rule() and new
DSL predicate agree for every rule on every evaluation, catching any
bridge divergence during development.

Adds test policy_rules_dsl_bridge_preserves_selected_rule_and_matches
which exercises a 3-rule config with overlapping matches to verify the
DSL bridge selects the same winner and reports the same matched set as
the prior implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(history): replace broken context menu portal with inline action buttons

The three-dot context menu in NavigatorHistory was non-functional across 6+
iterations due to compounding issues:

- Svelte 5 event delegation: onclick handlers share a document-level listener;
  e.stopPropagation() between two delegated handlers in the same pass is
  unreliable — the outer row onclick cleared contextMenuId immediately after
  the trigger set it.
- CSS animation stacking context trap: entrance keyframes with `to { transform:
  translateY(0) }` persist a stacking context via fill-mode:both. The Navigator
  grid cell has overflow:hidden, which clipped any position:fixed portal inside
  the component tree.
- Svelte portal placement: a component's {#if} blocks render as DOM children of
  the mounting point — they cannot escape the navigator's overflow:hidden grid
  cell regardless of z-index.

Fix: replaced the entire floating dropdown/portal system with two compact inline
icon buttons (retry=cyan, delete=red) that appear on row hover. No portal, no
position:fixed, no stacking context issues.

Also fix CSS animation `to` states: removed identity transforms (translateY(0),
scale(1)) from all entrance keyframe end states. With fill-mode:both these persisted
as permanent stacking contexts that trapped position:fixed descendants inside any
animated ancestor.

Also fix: use SvelteKit replaceState() from $app/navigation instead of
window.history.replaceState() to suppress router conflict warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(runtime_compat): replace remaining Cx::for_testing() with Cx::for_request() in production paths

Continue the Cx::for_request() migration started in aa703c26 by
replacing all remaining Cx::for_testing() call-sites in non-test
production code across frankenterm-core:

- ipc.rs: mpsc_recv_next helper
- native_events.rs: dispatch_event_with_timeout channel reserve
- pool.rs: Pool::try_acquire and Pool::acquire
- replay.rs: Player pause-loop watch receive
- runtime.rs: recv_event helper
- snapshot_engine.rs: interval and threshold scheduler loops
- storage.rs: WriteCommandSender::send and writer_loop recv
- tailer.rs: reserve_capture_event_permit macro
- vendored/mux_client.rs: PaneOutputSubscription::next and
  subscribe_pane_output subscription loop
- vendored/mux_pool.rs: MuxPool::acquire_client and
  execute_with_recovery_with_cx

Cx::for_testing() is now restricted to #[cfg(test)] paths only.
Cx::for_request() provides the correct production capability context
for request-scoped async operations.

commit.message:
fix: clear_activity signal prevents stale tags bleeding into broad new searches

When a user follows a specific activity search (e.g. ballet) with a broad
category query ("all girls overnight camps"), the session was retaining the
old activity tags, causing CSSL to add an unintended activity filter.

New clear_activity boolean in IntentResult:
- Gemini sets it true for broad, self-contained searches with no specific
  activity (type/gender/cost but no tags)
- session_manager clears accumulated tags/exclude_tags when true
- Distinct from recognized=False (which also clears type); this preserves
  the new type/gender params while clearing stale activity context

commit.message:
Restructure TPC data: separate temporal and static inputs

- Data generation saves temporal [T, 174] and static [381] separately
  instead of tiling flat+diagnoses across timesteps
- RNN_V2 and TCN gain static_dim parameter: when >0, accept (temporal,
  static) tuple input, embed diagnoses (316→20), and fuse with temporal
  representation before classification
- process_batch preserves tuple structure for two-input models
- Tree models extract last-timestep values + summary stats + raw static
  features (total ~729 dims per patient)
- Graph node feature modes (static, ds_static) extract static directly
  from tuple data format
- eICU_tpc family: 2 LSTMs (h=64, h=32) + 1 TCN (f=32), all with
  static fusion; eICU_tpc_mixed adds TCN (f=16) + XGBoost + RandomForest
- XGBoost defaults: max_depth=4, n_estimators=100

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(audit): add X-Subject-Id and X-Subject-Type fallback headers (#168)

X-Blaxel-Subject-Id and X-Blaxel-Subject-Type remain priority;
X-Subject-Id and X-Subject-Type are used as fallbacks when the
Blaxel headers are absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix formatter for msg

* The null-byte placeholder was always escaped by logrus's needsQuoting check,
causing both bytes.Replace calls to silently fail and leaving placeholder text
in every log line. Replace with a post-format regex that finds unquoted msg=
values and wraps them with strconv.Quote.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire epochId generation, prevB64 proof chaining, and timestamps into OCC proof pipeline

- Enclave boot: generate epochId = SHA-256(publicKeyB64 + ":" + bootNonceB64)
- handleCommit: include epochId in every proof's commit field (signed)
- handleCommit: chain proofs via prevB64 = SHA-256(canonicalize(prevProof))
- Constructor: accept optional epochId, include in commit fields
- types: add timestamps (RFC 3161 TSA) as top-level OCCProof field
- server: place TSA results in proof.timestamps.artifact (not metadata)
- key/health/init responses now include epochId
- Add docs site (index.html, whitepaper)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(experts): add fallback cascade for expert execution on rate-limit (#1532)

When an expert hits a rate limit on its primary CLI, the system now:
1. Detects rate-limit errors via pattern matching on the error message
2. Resolves the fallback chain for the expert's role category
3. Creates a temporary expert with the first fallback CLI adapter
4. Retries execution once with the fallback

Also adds getExpertFallbackChain() to create-expert-routing.ts for
resolving category-aware fallback chains (uses CATEGORY_CHAIN_OVERRIDES).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ui): built-in font system with font() API (#1079) (#1103)

* feat(ui): add built-in font system with font() API (#1079)

Introduce font() descriptor function and compileFonts() for first-class
font support in the theme system. Fonts are declared once and the framework
generates @font-face CSS, --font-* custom properties, and <link rel="preload">
tags automatically.

- font() creates branded FontDescriptor with weight, style, display, src, fallback
- compileFonts() generates @font-face blocks, CSS vars, and preload tags
- Theme integration: defineTheme({ fonts: { sans } }) merges font vars into :root
- SSR integration: preload tags injected into <head> before CSS
- Production SSR handler: inlineCSS support with XSS-safe escaping
- Shared sanitize.ts: sanitizeCssValue() and escapeHtmlAttr() utilities
- woff2-only validation: throws for non-woff2 src paths
- Landing page migrated from manual 46-line boilerplate to font() API
- Full test coverage: 23 font tests, 25 theme tests, SSR integration tests
- Type-level tests: font.test-d.ts validates all type constraints
- Subpath exports updated for font and compileFonts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): harden font/sanitize security and narrow types (#1103)

- sanitizeCssValue: strip <> and single quotes to prevent style tag breakout
- escapeHtmlAttr: escape single quotes (')
- FontDescriptor: narrow style to 'normal' | 'italic', display to union type
- compileFonts: validate font keys match /^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$/
- Add comprehensive sanitize.test.ts
- Add </style> breakout and key validation tests to font.test.ts

Initial commit: Build-a-Web student learning hub

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

BFP8_B weight cache: skip CPU packing on subsequent loads (~6x faster startup)

First run packs BF16→BFP8_B as before and writes 7.9GB cache file alongside model.
Subsequent runs read pre-packed data from cache (7.2s) instead of re-reading 18GB
of BF16 weights from GGUF and CPU-packing them (29s). Total startup drops from
~64s to ~11s. Cache invalidates automatically on GGUF file size/mtime change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add cargo-fuzz infrastructure for robustness testing

Three fuzz targets exercise the main input-parsing surfaces:
- fuzz_srt: SRT subtitle parser with subtitle lookup
- fuzz_parse_time: time string parser with format round-trip
- fuzz_probe: ffmpeg demuxer probe against arbitrary bytes

Extracts parse_srt_content(&str) from parse_srt(path) so the SRT
fuzzer can feed strings directly without temp file overhead. Adds
src/lib.rs to expose modules to the fuzz crate.

All three targets passed 5-minute runs (38M+ total executions, zero crashes).

commit.message:
fix: resolve 3 migration errors from production run

1. Migration 009 — remove indexes on views
   sold_by_sku and inventory_virtual are VIEWs, not base tables.
   ERROR 42809: cannot create index on relation — fixed.
   Replaced with indexes on the underlying wix_inventory_live base table.

2. Migrations 007, 010 — fix wrong column name
   inventory_virtual.available_qty does not exist.
   ERROR 42703: column does not exist — fixed.
   Correct column name is available_quantity throughout.

3. Migration 011 — cost coverage diagnostic
   New script to diagnose the 23.1% cost match rate:
   - Step A/B/C: identify unmatched orders and why
   - Step 2: run backfill_order_units() to fix unit='unknown' rows
   - Step 3: re-check match rate after normalization
   - Step 4: list remaining strain gaps with suggested cost SKU format
   The 23.1% rate is expected before migration 008 normalizes units.
   After running 008, the three-step strain+unit fallback should fire
   for previously-unknown units.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: migrate CI secrets to Doppler and fix Sentry release format (#209)

* chore: add missing secrets to Doppler migration

* chore: inline migration workflow for secrets context access

* chore: migrate GitHub Actions to Doppler secrets

Replace all ${{ secrets.X }} references in deploy.yml and
deploy-activity.yml with Doppler-fetched environment variables.
Only GITHUB_TOKEN (auto-provided), DOPPLER_TOKEN (bootstrap),
and CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN remain as GitHub secrets.

Delete migration workflows (migrate-secrets-to-doppler.yml,
doppler-push.yml) that are no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use namespaced release format for Sentry release health

Use `mythicplus-bot@<sha>` format instead of bare SHA so Sentry can
properly associate sessions and errors with releases. Pass `undefined`
when GIT_SHA is missing instead of the static string 'unknown'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use random delimiter for multiline secrets in GITHUB_ENV

Replace hardcoded DPLR_EOF with a random hex delimiter to prevent
silent truncation if any secret value contains the delimiter string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: fix lint errors for unused imports and variable

Remove unused `getPlayerList` import, `createRoleBoardEmbed` import,
and `botInstance` variable assignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: stop shadowing GITHUB_TOKEN and limit Doppler blast radius

Rename the PAT env var from GITHUB_TOKEN to GH_ISSUE_TOKEN in the
deploy step so it no longer shadows the Actions-provided token.
The remote deploy script still receives it as GITHUB_TOKEN.

Filter deploy-activity Doppler secrets to only export
DISCORD_APPLICATION_ID and VITE_FIREBASE_* keys instead of
loading the entire Doppler project.
